brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +58.8% since Feb 27 — Brent $112.78 (Mar 30 settle); nearing $120 Mar 31 after Al Salmi tanker attack off Dubai; +55% monthly — record since 1988; Goldman: could exceed $147
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 30 settle: $112.78/bbl — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988 (+~55%). Mar 31: nearing $120 again after Iranian drone struck Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai. Dubai physical $126/bbl — gap reflects acute physical supply stress. Goldman: if Hormuz at 5% flow for 10 weeks, daily Brent could exceed 2008 record of $147. Société Générale projects up to $150. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history of global oil market.' Apr 6 Hormuz deadline. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +54.7% since Feb 27 — WTI $102.88 (Mar 30 settle); +~53% monthly — best since May 2020; first close >$100 since July 2022; Apr 6 Hormuz deadline
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 30 settle: $102.88/bbl. WTI +~53% in March — best since May 2020. First close above $100 since July 2022. Gas crossed $4.00/gal nationally (Mar 31). Diesel $5.42/gal. California $5.88/gal. Brent-WTI spread ~$10. Al Salmi tanker struck at Dubai port Mar 31 — oil surging toward $120. Apr 6 Hormuz deadline. Jones Act waiver under consideration. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.02 / +34.2% since Feb 27 — crossed $4.00 nationally Mar 31 — highest since 2022 — CA $5.88 — diesel $5.42 — Jones Act waiver considered
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Mar 31: crossed $4.00/gal nationally — highest since 2022. Up $1.02/gal in one month. California leads at $5.88/gal. Diesel hit $5.42/gal nationally. Trump admin considering Jones Act waiver to ease distribution. Al Salmi tanker attack at Dubai port Mar 31 adds further upward pressure. Source: AAA / EIA / multiple outlets.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -535 pts / -7.8% — S&P ~6,344 (Mar 30 close); 5 consecutive weekly losses; VIX 30.61; Moody's recession prob 49%; 10Y volatile 4.31-4.43%
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 30 close: ~6,344 — down ~9.4% from January peak. Five consecutive weekly losses — longest in four years. Dow ~45,500 (Mar 31 intraday). Nasdaq ~20,950. VIX 30.61. 10-Year yield extreme volatility Mar 31: swinging 12bps between 4.31% and 4.43%. Moody's AI recession model at 49% probability. Goldman year-end target 7,600; Evercore sees bottom ~6,150. XOM +28% YTD at $171. CVX near 52-week high $210.71. DAL $64 and UAL $91.35 down 15-20% monthly. Gold $4,578-4,600/oz. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / Moody's.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — Al Salmi tanker attacked at Dubai — Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint scenario — $200B supplemental — Apr 6 deadline
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 32: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. Al Salmi tanker struck by Iranian drone at Port of Dubai Mar 31. Houthi entry (Mar 28) threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure — dual chokepoint scenario unprecedented in modern history. First 100 hours: ~$3.7B (CSIS). Day 12: $16.5B. $200B supplemental request faces bipartisan resistance. National debt $39T. 82nd Airborne deploying. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Penn Wharton / WaPo.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (32 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$38.1B — $200B supplemental + $839B FY2026 + $156B reconciliation = $1T+ direct military this year
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 32: ~$38.1B. Pentagon $200B supplemental at ~$1B/day — faces significant bipartisan resistance. If approved alongside $839B FY2026 defense budget and $156B reconciliation, direct military spending exceeds $1 trillion this year. National debt at record $39 trillion. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / The Hill / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 11,000+ US targets as of Mar 28 — 140+ naval vessels destroyed — 8,000+ combat flights — 'largest elimination of a navy since WWII' — Yazd yellowcake + Khondab struck
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 28): 11,000+ US targets struck, up from 8,000 on Day 22. 8,000+ combat flights. 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed — 'largest elimination of a navy since World War II.' GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators against underground coastal missile silos. A-10 Thunderbolts and AH-64 Apaches for anti-surface warfare since Mar 19. Day 32: US-Israeli airstrikes hit Yazd yellowcake facility and Khondab Heavy Water Complex (Mar 28). At least 3 projectiles near Bushehr NPP. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~31,000+ through Day 32. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Axios / DoD.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 US KIA confirmed — 6 Kuwait drone attack (Mar 1) + 6 KC-135 crash (Mar 12) + 1 Saudi strike — 290 WIA (255 RTD, 10 seriously) — 82nd Airborne deploying
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 US KIA confirmed: 6 soldiers from 103rd ESC killed in Iranian drone strike near Camp Arifjan, Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 airmen killed when KC-135 tanker crashed in western Iraq (Mar 12); 1 from injuries (Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington). 290 wounded (255 returned to duty; 10 remain seriously wounded). Damage to 17+ US military/diplomatic sites exceeding $800M. Iran destroyed $300M E-3 Sentry at Prince Sultan AB. 82nd Airborne deploying ~2,000-4,000 paratroopers. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 290 WIA (255 RTD, 10 seriously) — 17+ US sites damaged ($800M+) — 50,000+ deployed — Tripoli ARG arrived — Boxer en route — 82nd Airborne deploying
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 290 WIA per CENTCOM (255 returned to duty; 10 remain seriously wounded). Iranian strikes have damaged 17+ US military/diplomatic sites ($800M+ in damage). Iran destroyed $300M E-3 Sentry radar aircraft at Prince Sultan AB. Many facilities 'nearly uninhabitable.' 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (~3,500) arrived Mar 27. Boxer ARG/11th MEU departed San Diego, en route. 82nd Airborne (2,000-4,000 from 1st BCT) deploying. USS George H.W. Bush in transit. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / USNI Fleet Tracker.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Deputy Health Minister: 1,937 killed, 24,800+ wounded — HRANA: 3,389 (1,527 civilians, 228+ children) — 226 medical units damaged — 100,000+ fled Tehran
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iran Deputy Health Minister: 1,937 killed, 24,800+ wounded. HRANA (independent): 3,389 killed including 1,527 civilians and 228+ children. Health Ministry: 226 medical/health units damaged, 12 hospitals completely non-operational. Red Crescent: 87,000+ non-military structures damaged, ~600 educational centers attacked. 100,000+ fled Tehran in first two days. Estimated aggregate including military: ~7,200. Iran launched 'Janfada' volunteer recruitment. Multiple parliamentarians pushing NPT withdrawal legislation. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Red Crescent / OCHA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,200+ killed (incl. 124+ children) — over 1M displaced (~20% of population) — 10 Israeli soldiers killed — IDF reached Litani tributary — ground invasion since Mar 16
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,200+ killed per Lebanese MoH (including 124+ children). Over 1 million displaced (~20% of population). 10 Israeli soldiers killed. Israel launched ground invasion Mar 16; forces reached Litani River tributary by Mar 31. Netanyahu ordered 'further expand' buffer zone toward Litani. DM Katz: displaced Lebanese 'completely prevented' from returning. 4 hospitals and 50+ primary healthcare centers closed. UNHCR requested $61 million. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1.2M+ (quarter of population) — 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled — global humanitarian aid disrupted
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced. Lebanon: over 1.2 million (~25% of population). 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled from Iran. Global humanitarian aid disrupted: WFP food for Afghan children stuck, WHO medicine for Gaza held up, IRC supplies for Sudan stranded in Dubai. Dubai's International Humanitarian City effectively crippled. Shipping containers face $3,000 emergency surcharge. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / WaPo / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman — ~20,000 seafarers waiting — insurance 5%+ of vessel value — Spain closes airspace to US planes
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman with ~20,000 seafarers waiting. Insurance rates surged from 0.25-0.5% to 5%+ of vessel value per transit. Iran 'tollbooth' system charging ~$2M per transit in Chinese yuan. Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among our options' — dual chokepoint scenario. Spain closed airspace to US planes involved in Iran attacks (Mar 30). Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's List / S&P Global.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 228+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 124 + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school: 100+ schoolchildren — UNHRC investigating — 600+ educational centers attacked
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 228+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 124 children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Minab school (Feb 28): 100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12 killed — UN Human Rights Council examining. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. Nearly 25 million people in affected countries were already refugees or displaced before February escalation. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC.
